title: Intel's Andrew Grove
html:
TL;DR

  • Jan 8 2022, 5:37pm Apple Note: title "Intels Andrew grove", body empty. Captured-but-abandoned reading-list / mentor-flag note.
  • Andy Grove = Intel's legendary CEO, author of Only the Paranoid Survive and High Output Management — canonical mentor-from-afar for any operator-curious 20-year-old. Yahir tagging him is a clean signal of the early-2022 reading orbit.
  • Created 3 weeks after the Dec 27 2021 "What's Now" reset — the OSU-freshman intellectual stretch is still active. Slots into the same reading-anchor mode as the Nov 2021 Ram Dass / Sisyphus note.
  • Empty-body form — title-as-bookmark. Sibling to dozens of empty-body entries; the move is "capture this name, fill in later", then the later never happens.
  • Speculative graph link: Grove's strategic inflection point concept maps cleanly onto Yahir's later disruption / scenario thinking frames. If Yahir has actually read Grove since, it's a missing chain in the cluster.

Source quote

"Intels Andrew grove"

(Body: empty.)

Open questions

  • Has Yahir actually read Andy Grove since Jan 2022, or is this still aspirational?
  • Strategic inflection point — does it appear in any later 2024–2026 strategy notes? If yes, link retroactively. If no, the bookmark died on the page.
  • Should Andy Grove become an entity stub now? Borderline: single mention in vault, but a high-significance proper-noun (named CEO + canonical book author) — under schema-v2 the "clearly significant" gate applies. Stubbing as andy-grove.md.
